title: देवी माहात्म्यम् उत्तम चरित्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री देवी माहात्म्यम् - उत्तम चरित्रम् ॥

ॐ नमश्चण्डिकायै॥

ध्यानम् -
घण्टाशूलहलानि शङ्खमुसले चक्रं धनुः सायकं
हस्ताब्जैर्दधतीं घनान्तविलसच्छीतांशुतुल्यप्रभाम्।
गौरीदेहसमुद्भवां त्रिजगतामाधारभूतां महा-
पूर्वामत्र सरस्वतीमनुभजे शुम्भादिदैत्यार्दिनीम्॥

ॐ क्लीं शुम्भनिशुम्भयोर्वधार्थम् -

पुनरेवाभवत्पापौ शुम्भनिशुम्भावसुरौ।
त्रैलोक्यमक्रामताञ्च देवेभ्योऽपहृतश्रियौ॥१॥

ततो देवाः स्तुतिं चक्रुर्देवीं हिमवत्पार्श्वगाम्।
या देवी सर्वभूतेषु शक्तिरूपेण संस्थिता॥२॥

नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः।
या देवी सर्वभूतेषु बुद्धिरूपेण संस्थिता॥३॥

नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः।
या देवी सर्वभूतेषु लक्ष्मीरूपेण संस्थिता॥४॥

नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥

॥ धूम्रलोचनवधः ॥

ततः कोपं चकारोग्रं अम्बिका तानरीन्प्रति।
कोपेन चास्या वदनं मषीवर्णमभूत्तदा॥५॥

भ्रुकुटीकुटिलात्तस्या ललाटफलकाद्द्रुतम्।
काली करालवदना विनिष्क्रान्तासिपाशिनी॥६॥

विचित्रखट्वाङ्गधरा नरमालाविभूषणा।
द्वीपिचर्मपरीधाना शुष्कमांसातिभैरवा॥७॥

अतिविस्तारवदना जिह्वाललनभीषणा।
निमग्नारक्तनयना नादापूरितदिङ्मुखा॥८॥

सा वेगेनाभिपतिता घातयन्ती महासुरान्।
सैन्ये तत्र सुरारीणां धूम्रलोचनं जघान ह॥९॥

॥ चण्डमुण्डवधः ॥

ततश्चण्डश्च मुण्डश्च बलैः परिवृतावुभौ।
युयुधाते तथा कालीं शरशक्तिपरश्वधैः॥१०॥

जगृहे चण्डस्य केशेषु शिरस्तस्य खड्गिना।
मुण्डमप्यतिनक्षत्रं शरेण निजघान सा॥११॥

ततो हतौ दृष्ट्वा चण्डमुण्डौ तथा बलम्।
चण्डमुण्डवधात्तस्याश्चामुण्डेति ततोऽभिधाम्॥१२॥

॥ रक्तबीजवधः ॥

रक्तबीजो महादैत्यो युयुधे चाम्बिकासखः।
तस्य यद्रक्तपातो भूत्तावद्दैत्यसम्भवः॥१३॥

ततस्तामाह चामुण्डां कालिकां चण्डिका मुदा।
चामुण्डे त्वं पिबास्यस्मात् रक्तमुत्पातितं महत्॥१४॥

ततः काली पपौ रक्तं रक्तबीजस्य सर्वशः।
निर्रक्तो महादैत्यः पपात भुवि पातितः॥१५॥

॥ निशुम्भवधः ॥

ततो निशुम्भो वेगेन गदामुद्यम्य चण्डिकाम्।
देवी तस्य शिरो देहात्खड्गेन निचकर्त ह॥१६॥

॥ शुम्भवधः ॥

ततः शुम्भो महादैत्यः क्रोधान्धो युयुधे तदा।
एकैवाहं जगत्यत्र द्वितीया का ममापरा।
पश्यैता दुष्ट मय्येव विशन्त्यो मद्विभूतयः॥१७॥

ततः समस्ताः समरे तस्यामेवाभवन्स्थिताः।
ब्राह्मणी माहेश्वरी कौमारी वैष्णवी तथा॥१८॥

वाराही नारसिंही च ऐन्द्री शक्तिः परायणम्।
देवीदेहात्समुत्पन्ना विनाशायासुरद्विषाम्॥१९॥

एकैव तदभूद्देवी स्वशरीराद्विनिर्गताः।
शक्तयो यास्समस्तास्ताः समाविश्य तनुं गताः॥२०॥

ततो देवी शूलेनैनं हृदि विव्याध दानवम्।
स गतासुश्च पपात मेदिन्यां महासुरः॥२१॥

ततः प्रसन्नमखिलं हते तस्मिन्दुरात्मनि।
जगत्स्वास्थ्यमतीवापि सगन्धर्वमभूत्तदा॥२२॥

॥ देवस्तुतिः ॥

देवा ऊचुः -
सर्वमंगलमाङ्गल्ये शिवे सर्वार्थसाधिके।
शरण्ये त्र्यम्बके गौरि नारायणि नमोऽस्तु ते॥२३॥

सृष्टिस्थितिविनाशानां शक्तिभूते सनातनि।
गुणाश्रये गुणमये नारायणि नमोऽस्तु ते॥२४॥

शरणागतदीनार्तपरित्राणपरायणे।
सर्वस्यार्तिहरे देवि नारायणि नमोऽस्तु ते॥२५॥

॥ देवीवरदानम् ॥

देव्युवाच -
वरं यत्कांक्षयध्वं वो यच्छाम्यहमसंशयम्।
जगतामुपकाराय यत्किञ्चित्प्रार्थ्यतां मया॥२६॥

इत्थं यदा यदा बाधा दानवोत्था भविष्यति।
तदा तदावतीर्याहं करिष्याम्यरिसंक्षयम्॥२७॥

॥ इति श्री मार्कण्डेयपुराणे देवीमाहात्म्ये उत्तमचरित्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Devi Mahatmyam - Uttama Charitram ||

Om Namashchandikayai.

Dhyanam -
Ghanta-shula-halani Shankha-musale Chakram Dhanuh Sayakam
Hastaabjair Dadhatim Ghananta-vilasach-chhitamshu-tulya-prabham.
Gauri-deha-samudbhavam Tri-jagatam Adhara-bhutam Maha-
purvam Atra Sarasvatim Anubhaje Shumbhadi-daitya-ardinim.

Om Klim Shumbha-nishumbhayor Vadhartham -

Punar Evabhavat Papau Shumbha-nishumbhav Asurau.
Trailokyam Akramatancha Devebhyo'pahrita-shriyau.||1||

Tato Devah Stutim Chakrur Devim Himavat-parshvagam.
Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Shakti-rupena Samsthita.||2||

Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.
Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Buddhi-rupena Samsthita.||3||

Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.
Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Lakshmi-rupena Samsthita.||4||

Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.

|| Dhumra-lochana-vadhah ||

Tatah Kopam Chakarogram Ambika Tan Arin Prati.
Kopena Chasya Vadanam Mashi-varnam Abhut Tada.||5||

Bhrukuti-kutilat Tasya Lalata-phalakad Drutam.
Kali Karala-vadana Vinishkranta-asi-pashini.||6||

Vichitra-khatvanga-dhara Nara-mala-vibhushana.
Dvipi-charma-paridhana Shushka-mamsa-ati-bhairava.||7||

Ati-vistara-vadana Jihva-lalana-bhishana.
Nimagnaa-rakta-nayana Nada-purita-ding-mukha.||8||

Sa Vegena-abhipatita Ghatayanti Mahasuraan.
Sainye Tatra Sura-rinam Dhumra-lochanam Jaghana Ha.||9||

|| Chanda-Munda-vadhah ||

Tatash Chandash Cha Mundash Cha Balaih Parivritav Ubhau.
Yuyudhate Tatha Kalim Shara-shakti-parashvadhaih.||10||

Jagrihe Chandasya Kesheshu Shiras Tasya Khadgina.
Mundam Apy Ati-nakshtram Sharena Nijaghana Sa.||11||

Tato Hatau Drishtva Chanda-mundau Tatha Balam.
Chanda-munda-vadhat Tasyash Chamundeti Tato'bhidham.||12||

|| Raktabija-vadhah ||

Raktabijo Mahadaityo Yuyudhe Chambika-sakhah.
Tasya Yad Rakta-pato Bhut Tavad Daitya-sambhavah.||13||

Tatas Tam Aha Chamundam Kalikam Chandika Muda.
Chamunde Tvam Pibasya-smat Raktam Utpatitam Mahat.||14||

Tatah Kali Papau Raktam Raktabijasya Sarvashah.
Nir-rakto Mahadaityah Papata Bhuvi Patitah.||15||

|| Nishumbha-vadhah ||

Tato Nishumbho Vegena Gadam Udyamya Chandikam.
Devi Tasya Shiro Dehat Khadgena Nichakarta Ha.||16||

|| Shumbha-vadhah ||

Tatah Shumbho Mahadaityah Krodhandho Yuyudhe Tada.
Ekaivaaham Jagaty Atra Dvitiya Ka Mamapara.
Pashyaita Dushta Mayyeva Vishantyo Mad-vibhutayah.||17||

Tatah Samastah Samare Tasyam Evabhavan Sthitah.
Brahmanai Maheshvari Kaumari Vaishnavi Tatha.||18||

Varahi Narasimhi Cha Aindri Shaktih Parayanam.
Devi-dehat Samutpanna Vinashayasura-dvisham.||19||

Ekaiva Tad Abhud Devi Sva-sharirad Vinirgatah.
Shaktayo Yas Samastas Tah Samavishya Tanum Gatah.||20||

Tato Devi Shulena Enam Hridi Vivyadha Danavam.
Sa Gatasush Cha Papata Medinyam Mahasurah.||21||

Tatah Prasannam Akhilam Hate Tasmin Duratmani.
Jagat Svasthyam Ativapi Sa-gandharvam Abhut Tada.||22||

|| Deva-stutih ||

Deva Uchuh -
Sarva-mangala-mangalye Shive Sarvartha-sadhike.
Sharanye Tryambake Gauri Narayani Namo'stu Te.||23||

Srishti-sthiti-vinashanam Shakti-bhute Sanatani.
Guna-ashraye Guna-maye Narayani Namo'stu Te.||24||

Sharanagata-dinarta-paritrana-paraayane.
Sarvasyarti-hare Devi Narayani Namo'stu Te.||25||

|| Devi-varadanam ||

Devy Uvacha -
Varam Yat Kankshyadhvam Vo Yachchhamyaham Ashamshayam.
Jagatam Upakaraya Yat-kinchit Prarthyatam Maya.||26||

Ittham Yada Yada Badha Danavottha Bhavishyati.
Tada Tada Avatiryaham Karishyamy Ari-samkshayam.||27||

|| Iti Shri Markandeya-purane Devi-mahatmye Uttama-charitram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
शुम्भ और निशुम्भ नामक दो महाअसुरों ने तीनों लोकों पर अधिकार कर लिया और देवताओं की श्री छीन ली। देवताओं ने हिमालय पर जाकर देवी की स्तुति की।

देवी के क्रोध से उनके ललाट से काली प्रकट हुईं जो करालवदना, खट्वांगधारिणी, नरमालाभूषिता थीं। काली ने धूम्रलोचन का वध किया।

फिर चण्ड और मुण्ड ने युद्ध किया। काली ने दोनों के शिर काटे। इससे उनका नाम चामुण्डा पड़ा।

रक्तबीज नामक दैत्य का रक्त जहाँ गिरता वहाँ नए दैत्य उत्पन्न होते। चण्डिका ने काली से कहा कि वह रक्त पी ले। काली ने सारा रक्त पी लिया और रक्तबीज मारा गया।

निशुम्भ और शुम्भ का भी वध हुआ। देवी ने कहा - मैं ही एक हूँ, मेरे अतिरिक्त दूसरी कोई नहीं। सभी शक्तियाँ मुझमें ही विलीन हैं। शूल से शुम्भ का हृदय विदीर्ण किया।

देवताओं ने स्तुति की - हे सर्वमंगलमांगल्ये! हे नारायणि! जब-जब दानवों का उत्पात होगा, तब-तब मैं अवतरित होकर शत्रुओं का संहार करूँगी।

viniyog.shloka: ॐ अस्य श्रीउत्तमचरित्रस्य रुद्र ऋषिः। अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। श्रीमहासरस्वती देवता। भीमा शक्तिः। भ्रामरी बीजम्। सूर्यस्तत्त्वम्। सामवेदः स्वरूपम्। श्रीमहासरस्वतीप्रीत्यर्थे उत्तमचरित्रपाठे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Contains the climactic battles against Shumbha, Nishumbha, and Raktabija
Recitation destroys sattva-bound ego and grants ultimate liberation
Invokes Kali/Chamunda for destruction of deeply rooted negative forces
Contains the famous 'Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu' universal praise verses
Devi's promise to incarnate whenever evil arises gives eternal assurance
Invokes supreme protection, victory, and spiritual empowerment
description: Dedicated to Goddess Durga, the Devi Mahatmyam Uttama Charitra (देवी माहात्म्यम् उत्तम चरित्रम्) is a sacred prayer revered in Hindu tradition. This revered text originates from Markandeya Purana (Devi Mahatmyam / Durga Saptashati). Devotees recite this prayer to devi' promise to incarnate whenever evil arises gives eternal assurance and grant supreme protection, victory, and spiritual empowerment. It is also said to recitation destroys sattva-bound ego and grant ultimate liberation. It is especially recommended on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Friday (Shukravar) and during Navratri for maximum spiritual benefit. With 27 verses and a reading time of about 30 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Devi Mahatmyam Uttama Charitra in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Devi Mahatmyam Uttama Charitra in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Shumbha-Nishumbha-Raktabija stories from Durga Saptashati Chapters 5-13.
relatedSearches: Devi Mahatmyam Uttama Charitra Sanskrit Hindi PDF
Durga Saptashati chapters 5 to 13 Shumbha Nishumbha vadh
Ya Devi Sarva Bhuteshu mantra Argala Stotram source
Raktabija vadha Kali drinks blood story Devi Mahatmya
Chanda Munda vadh Chamunda name origin
Saptamatrika Brahmani Maheshvari Kaumari Vaishnavi list
Aparajita Stuti Narayani Stuti Uttara Charitra
Kaushiki birth from Parvati kosha Devi Mahatmya
Uttama Charitra recitation Navratri Maha Navami

seoFaqs.question: What is the Devi Mahatmyam Uttama Charitra?
seoFaqs.answer: The Devi Mahatmyam Uttama Charitra (देवी माहात्म्यम् उत्तम चरित्रम् — 'the Final / Highest Episode') is the longest and most theologically dense section of the Devi Mahatmya / Durga Saptashati / Chandi Path, comprising chapters 5 through 13 of the 13-chapter text preserved in the Markandeya Purana. It narrates the third great Devi-charitra: the slaying of the demon-brothers Shumbha and Nishumbha and their generals Dhumralochana, Chanda, Munda and Raktabija, who had usurped the three worlds. The presiding deity (devata) of this charitra is Mahasaraswati, the rishi is Rudra, the chhand is Anushtup, the bija is Klim, the shakti is Bhrami, the tattva is Surya, and the svarupa is Yajurveda. The charitra contains some of the most beloved and most chanted hymns of the entire Hindu tradition — the Aparajita Stuti / Narayani Stuti, the Tantrokta Devi Suktam (Ya Devi sarva-bhuteshu), the Brahma Krita Devi Stotra, and the famous final boon: yada yada bhavishyati badha — 'whenever danger arises, I shall manifest to destroy the enemies'. (Source: Markandeya Purana, Devi Mahatmya chapters 5-13.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Shumbha-Nishumbha story in the Uttama Charitra?
seoFaqs.answer: After the Madhyama Charitra slaying of Mahishasura, two more powerful asura brothers — Shumbha and Nishumbha — defeated Indra and conquered svarga, seizing the rights of yajna, the sacred fires, and all svarga's wealth. The dispossessed devas went to the Himalayas and praised Devi with the celebrated Aparajita Stuti — Ya Devi sarva-bhuteshu shakti-rupena samsthita. Pleased, Devi emerged as Kaushiki — the dazzlingly beautiful goddess who arose from the kosha (sheath) of Parvati, leaving behind the dark-complexioned Kalika. Shumbha-Nishumbha's messenger Sugriva proposed marriage; Devi declared her vow that only one who defeated her in battle could win her hand. The asuras dispatched Dhumralochana (slain by a roar), then Chanda and Munda (slain by Kali, hence her name Chamunda), then Raktabija whose every drop of blood spawned new asuras (Kali drank his blood until he fell), then Nishumbha (beheaded by Devi's sword), and finally Shumbha (pierced through the heart by her shula). With his death, the three worlds were freed. (Source: Devi Mahatmya 5.1-10.40.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Uttama Charitra be recited for maximum benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: The Uttama Charitra is recited daily during all nine days of Sharad Navratri and Chaitra Navratri — typically over days 4-9 of the nine-day paath schedule, with chapters 11-13 (containing the Narayani Stuti and the Devi-vara-dana) read on Maha Navami and Vijayadashami (Dussehra). It is especially powerful on Maha Ashtami, Maha Navami, Vijayadashami, Tuesday (Mangalvar), Friday (Shukravar), every Purnima, Magha Purnima, on Lalita Panchami, on Saraswati Puja day (Vasant Panchami), and during Sata-Chandi or Sahasra-Chandi homa. The dhyana invokes the eight-armed Mahasaraswati holding ghanta, shula, hala (plough), shankha, musala, chakra, dhanus and sayaka — recited at dawn after a bath, facing east, before a Devi yantra or murti with white flowers (or red/yellow), with kumkum, akshata, fruits and a ghee diya. The Kavach, Argala, Kilaka, Navarna and Prathama-Madhyama charitras are recited first, then the Uttama Charitra. (Sources: Devi Mahatmya paath vidhi; Drikpanchang Navratri.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Aparajita Stuti / Ya Devi Sarva Bhuteshu mantra?
seoFaqs.answer: The Aparajita Stuti (Tantrokta Devi Suktam) appears at the start of the Uttama Charitra (Devi Mahatmya chapter 5). When Shumbha-Nishumbha conquered svarga, the devas went to the Himalayas and offered this celebrated stotra. It contains the most famous refrain in all of Devi worship: Ya Devi sarva-bhuteshu... rupena samsthita, namastasyai namastasyai namastasyai namo namah — 'Salutations again and again to that Devi who abides in all beings as ___'. The blank is filled successively with shakti (power), buddhi (intellect), Lakshmi (wealth), nidra (sleep), kshudha (hunger), chhaya (shadow), shanti (peace), shraddha (faith), kanti (radiance), tushti (contentment), matr (mother), bhranti (delusion), smriti (memory), daya (compassion), lajja (modesty), vritti (livelihood), jati (caste), and many more — declaring that every faculty in every being is a manifestation of the one Devi. The stuti is chanted daily in every Devi temple, at the start of Navratri, before any Chandi paath, and as a standalone stotra for invoking Devi's specific shaktis. (Source: Devi Mahatmya 5.7-79.)

seoFaqs.question: How does Kali get the name Chamunda in this charitra?
seoFaqs.answer: When Shumbha-Nishumbha sent their generals Chanda and Munda with a great army to capture Devi by force, Devi's brow furrowed in wrath. From the dark frown of her forehead emerged Kali — kala-vadana (terrible-faced), wearing a tiger skin and a garland of human heads, holding the khatvanga (skull-staff), with skeletal frame, lolling tongue and sunken red eyes. Kali fell upon the asura army, devouring elephants whole, crushing chariots in her teeth, and finally seized Chanda by the hair and severed his head with a single sword stroke. Then with a sharp arrow she beheaded Munda. She presented both heads to Chandika as a war-trophy. Pleased, Devi declared: yasmach Chandam cha Mundam cha grihitva tvam upagata, Chamundeti tato loke khyata devi bhavishyasi — 'because you have brought me Chanda and Munda, henceforth in the world you will be famed as Chamunda'. This is the canonical scriptural origin of the name Chamunda for the wrathful Kali-form of Devi — one of the Saptamatrikas. (Source: Devi Mahatmya 7.5-25.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the famous Devi-vara-dana at the end of the Uttama Charitra?
seoFaqs.answer: After slaying Shumbha and freeing the three worlds, Devi grants the celebrated Devi-vara-dana to the devas in chapter 12 of the Devi Mahatmya. She declares: ittham yada yada badha danavottha bhavishyati, tada tada avatirya aham karishyamy ari-samkshayam — 'whenever danger arises from demonic forces, I shall manifest myself in that age to destroy the enemies'. She then enumerates her future avataras: the Vindhyavasini who slew Vipra-chitti, the Raktadantika, the hundred-eyed Shatakshi who would arise during a hundred-year drought, the Shakambhari who would feed all beings, the Durga who would slay Durgama, the Bhima Devi of the Himalayas who would slay Aruna, and the Bhramari Devi who would slay Aruna. The chapter also contains the famous phala-shruti declaring the fruits of reciting the Saptashati during sandhi-puja, on Ashtami-Navami, on Chaturdashi, on solar/lunar eclipses, and on the death-anniversary of an enemy — promising freedom from disease, victory in war, removal of grahas, fulfilment of all desires and ultimate moksha. (Source: Devi Mahatmya 11.40-12.40.)
